question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a malformed operator accountId string (e.g., '0.0.' with no third segment) to trigger an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException in the split/length check at startup, causing the monitor to fail to initialize and freeze all outbound transaction submission indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged user inject a crafted accountId value like '0.0.2.extra' that passes the split length check but causes Long.valueOf() to throw NumberFormatException, crashing the PostConstruct phase and blocking all transaction processing for the duration of the outage?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged user provide an accountId whose shard/realm segments contain non-numeric characters (e.g., 'abc.def.2') to cause Long.valueOf() to throw NumberFormatException inside init(), halting the monitor startup and freezing all network transaction routing for 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged user set the operator accountId to a value with mismatched shard/realm that is not equal to DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID ('0.0.2'), triggering the IllegalArgumentException branch in init() and causing the monitor to refuse to start, effectively freezing all transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged user craft an operator accountId of exactly '0.0.2' but with a shard/realm mismatch in commonProperties, causing init() to call operator.setAccountId() with a malformed format string that breaks downstream transaction signing and delays block processing beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged user set the mirrorNode field to null and set network to HederaNetwork.OTHER, causing getMirrorNode() to return a MirrorNodeProperties with an empty-string host, making all mirror node REST/gRPC calls fail and freezing transaction confirmation for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged user leave mirrorNode as null and set network to HederaNetwork.OTHER so that getMirrorNode() returns a MirrorNodeProperties whose host is an empty string, causing all downstream gRPC and REST calls to fail with connection errors and stalling transaction block processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged user inject a mirrorNode configuration pointing to a non-existent or attacker-controlled host, causing getMirrorNode() to return a poisoned MirrorNodeProperties that routes all transaction status queries to a black-hole endpoint, delaying block confirmation beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.frequency] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.frequency to its minimum allowed value (30 seconds) to cause the monitor to re-validate all nodes every 30 seconds, consuming all available threads and delaying actual transaction submission by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.maxAttempts] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.maxAttempts to its maximum integer value, causing each node validation to retry indefinitely and exhaust all maxThreads (25), blocking transaction submission threads and freezing network transactions for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.maxBackoff] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.maxBackoff to its maximum allowed value (10 seconds) combined with a high maxAttempts, causing cumulative retry delays that push total node validation time beyond 500% of average block time and freeze transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.retryBackoff] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.retryBackoff to Duration.ofMinutes(2) (the default) combined with a large maxAttempts, causing the monitor to stall transaction routing for multiple minutes per failed node, exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.requestTimeout to a very large value, causing each node validation request to block for an extended period, consuming all maxThreads and delaying transaction submission beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.maxNodes] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.maxNodes to 1 so that only one node is ever validated, and if that node is slow or unreachable, all transaction submission is routed to a single bottleneck, causing block delays exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.maxThreads] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.maxThreads to 1, serializing all node validation work onto a single thread, causing a queue of pending validations that delays transaction routing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.enabled] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.enabled to false, disabling all node health checks so that unhealthy or attacker-controlled nodes remain in the active set, causing transaction submissions to be routed to non-responsive nodes and freezing block processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.retrieveAddressBook] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.retrieveAddressBook to false, preventing the monitor from fetching the current network address book, causing it to use a stale or empty node set and freezing all transaction submission for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.tls] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.tls to TlsMode.TLS when all nodes only expose plaintext port 50211, causing all node validation attempts to fail (since TlsMode.TLS only accepts port 50212), marking all nodes as invalid and freezing transaction submission for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeValidationProperties.tls] Can an unprivileged user set nodeValidation.tls to TlsMode.PLAINTEXT when all nodes only expose TLS port 50212, causing all node validation attempts to be filtered out by the PLAINTEXT predicate (port != 50212), resulting in zero valid nodes and freezing all transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeProperties.getEndpoint()] Can an unprivileged user inject a node host value of 'in-process:fake' into the nodes set, causing getEndpoint() to return an in-process stub address that never resolves to a real network node, routing all transactions to a dead endpoint and freezing block processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeProperties.getEndpoint()] Can an unprivileged user set a node's host to an unresolvable domain name, causing getEndpoint() to return a host:port string that always results in DNS resolution failures, stalling all transaction submissions to that node and contributing to block delays exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeProperties.getNodeId()] Can an unprivileged user set a node's accountId to a value where accountId.num - 3 produces a negative nodeId, causing the monitor to reference a non-existent node in the network topology and routing transactions to an invalid target, freezing block processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeProperties.toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged user set a node's certHash to a crafted value that causes ByteString.copyFromUtf8() to produce an invalid certificate hash, causing TLS handshake failures during node validation and freezing all TLS-based transaction submissions for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeProperties.toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged user inject a node host that is a valid IP address but belongs to a black-hole network, causing toNodeAddress() to build a ServiceEndpoint with an unreachable IP, routing all transactions to a dead endpoint and delaying block processing beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: NodeProperties.toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged user set a node's host to a domain name that resolves to a loopback address (127.0.0.1), causing toIpAddressV4() to return a loopback IP in the ServiceEndpoint, routing all transactions to localhost and freezing network transaction processing for 500% or more of average block time?
